mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year industrial production growth (INDPRO) captures real output expansion; higher growth increases expected corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5 percent, the restrictiveness of monetary policy begins to weigh on equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment (UNRATE) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signal stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and consumer purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of UNRATE and CPIAUCSL does not produce negative returns in at least 55% of top-quartile interaction readings over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals impending demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months, PAYEMS deceleration does not coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Advance retail sales (RSAFS) growth directly reflects consumer spending strength, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to positively predict 5-day forward returns with a t-statistic exceeding 1.5 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts (HOUST) level below 1,200 thousand units signals insufficient residential investment and broader demand weakness, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,200, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) identifies a monetary-policy-induced supply contraction that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term does not yield negative 60-day forward returns in the top interaction quartile for more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating by more than 0.5 percent over a 3-month period triggers expectations of tighter Federal Reserve policy, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 0.5, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.
